metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section that captures (1) the content-triggered overlay dispatch pattern and (2) the Pass-2 domain-agnosticism contract, so future contributors have a durable, in-repo record of both decisions.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel pipeline stage (content-triggered overlay dispatch) and a firm architectural contract ('generic Pass-2 verifier is never taught a single domain's rubric') but does not mention capturing either in an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design doc. Future contributors will have no durable record of why overlays are triggered by content or why Pass-2 must stay domain-agnostic.
findings.location: Context — 'Governing principle (locked with user)'; AC — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped'
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/NNNN-overlay-dispatch-and-pass2-isolation.md (or a CLAUDE.md section) recording: the decision, the governing principle, the alternatives considered (dso's compensating adjudicators), and the consequences. Reference it from the relevant prompt/overlay files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism for making trailer-referenced ticket data available to the scope-intent overlay: either extend DiffContext/assemble_diff to accept and pass through ticket_ids (fetched by the caller), or clarify that the overlay operates on commit message/trailer text already present in the diff context and does not require full ticket bodies.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the scope-intent overlay 'checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets', but the code-review gate's DiffContext carries only diff text and changed files — no ticket metadata. The mechanism for making trailer-referenced ticket data available inside the overlay prompt is unspecified, leaving the AC mechanically incomplete.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'content-triggered overlay capability' extends one of the two existing trigger paths (deterministic glob / LLM-escalated from ADR 0010) or introduces a distinct third path, and — if the latter — justify the new pattern against the established approach.
findings.finding: The 'content-triggered overlay capability' is described as a new capability to ship (AC: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped; deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it'), but the mechanism is unspecified and unjustified against the existing overlay trigger system (ADR 0010: deterministic glob-triggers + capped LLM escalation). The existing system already supports both deterministic triggers (T5a/T5d/T7/T12 in plan-review) and LLM-routed overlays — what makes 'content-triggered' distinct, why a new capability is needed rather than extending the existing pattern, and whether this adds a third trigger path or replaces one of the two existing ones is not addressed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped; deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise Success Criterion 2 to specify: (a) the corpus or eval suite against which recall and FP-noise are measured, (b) the baseline snapshot (e.g. pre-change eval run), and (c) a minimum threshold or direction (e.g. 'recall ≥ X% on the seeded eval suite; FP rate no higher than baseline') so the criterion is evaluable via a deterministic command in-session.
findings.finding: Success Criterion 2 ('Measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in advisory nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline') lacks a defined baseline, measurement method, threshold, and test corpus — making it impossible to evaluate in-session and non-falsifiable without additional context.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in advisory nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline, without any new source of BLOCK...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a concrete measurement protocol for the 'measurable increase' criterion: specify what dataset/eval harness produces the baseline, which metrics are tracked (e.g., recall@N, FP rate), and what delta constitutes success (even a directional 'must not regress' floor counts).
findings.finding: The Success Criteria promise a 'measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in advisory nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline,' but the plan specifies no baseline measurement, no target metric value, and no mechanism for taking the post-change measurement. Without these, the criterion is not end-state-observable — there is no way to declare the epic done on this dimension.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in advisory nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a single owner story or merge order for criteria_routing.json edits (e.g., f983 first as the seam story, then da25, then 3c5d) and document this in the epic notes or each child's scope.
findings.finding: Three children (da25, f983, 3c5d) all modify criteria_routing.json with no stated coordination protocol, creating implicit shared state that risks merge conflicts and inconsistent routing configuration.
findings.location: child da25-8af4-7c15-4b4e; child f983-9441-35f4-445f; child 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to each of da25, f983, and 3c5d: 'criteria_routing.json edit must be sequenced after [prior owner]; coordinate via a shared routing PR or a designated integration story.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion (or extend AC #1) that requires capturing a pre-adoption baseline recall/FP-rate and a post-adoption comparison report, so the 'measurable increase' Success Criterion is verifiable.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria promise 'Measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in advisory nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline' but no Acceptance Criterion covers establishing that baseline or capturing/reporting the delta — the measurement commitment is orphaned.
findings.location: Success Criteria — second bullet; Acceptance Criteria (all items)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'A before/after eval report exists comparing valid-finding recall and advisory-nit/FP rate on a shared golden-set between the pre-adoption and post-adoption prompts; the post-adoption numbers meet or exceed the SC targets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign one child (or add an AC to 1669) to capture the current-gate baseline metrics (advisory-vs-blocking ratio, findings/run, verify-drop rate) before any prompt change lands, so the post-epic delta can be computed.
findings.finding: SC-B ('measurable increase in valid-finding recall and reduction in nit/FP noise vs. the current gate baseline') is not covered by any child: no child commits to capturing the pre-change baseline metrics or measuring a post-change delta against it. 1669 emits health-band metrics going forward but does not establish a before/after comparison.
findings.location: child 1669-a499-e540-46a4 (closest owner); parent Success Criteria SC-B
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to 1669: 'Baseline snapshot of coverage.metrics captured before any prompt story merges; post-epic delta documented against that snapshot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the shared scope-intent criterion vocabulary as a named, versioned contract (e.g., a shared constants file or schema) and add an AC to 3c5d requiring that the overlay's criterion names match the canonical source.
findings.finding: Child 3c5d introduces a residual cross-system coupling to plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary ('Reuses plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary') with no versioning or stability guarantee, meaning a vocabulary change in plan-review could silently break the code-review scope-intent overlay's finding taxonomy without any alert.
findings.location: child 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 3c5d ACs: 'Scope-intent criterion vocabulary sourced from a shared canonical definition (not copied); a lint/test confirms vocabulary alignment with plan-review at merge time.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend f93a's scope to include at least minimal eval specs (one recall + one no-fire case each) for all six overlays, or split into a separate eval-foundation-overlays child; do not leave four overlays' eval foundation solely inside da8a.
findings.finding: f93a's eval foundation explicitly covers only 'at least the tests+security overlays', leaving the eval foundation for the other four specialist overlays (performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) split across da8a's own ACs only — without a foundation-level spec. The parent AC1 requires 'eval foundation for the base/overlay/verify prompts', implying all overlays need a foundation, not just two.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c; child da8a-75f5-ccb2-4b5f
findings.suggested_fix: Update f93a AC to: 'Eval specs exist for base, verify, and all six overlays (tests, security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts); at least one recall and one no-fire case per overlay.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Link or cite the specific experiment that backs the positive linter-deference line, including its scope conditions and data, so the AC is traceable and the 'only' constraint is justified.
findings.finding: The AC 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only' references an experiment by its conclusion ('experiment-backed') but does not cite or link the experiment, its data, or its scope conditions. The decision to carry 'only' the positive linter-deference line (as opposed to broader deference rules) is a calibration choice that should be traceable to a specific experiment — especially since the plan explicitly requires eval cases 'seeded from dso's documented FP incidents and defect classes' for other changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an eval AC to 7c58 (or extend f93a) requiring at least one recall case (change-detector test → delete-bad-test) and one no-fire case (legitimate test improvement does not trigger delete-bad-test) per move.
findings.finding: SC-A ('every prompt/detector change lands with a passing eval') is not covered for child 7c58 (coach moves): 7c58's acceptance criteria contain no eval requirement, and f93a seeds no coach-move eval cases. Coach move templates are prompt/template artifacts subject to the same regression risk the parent's eval gate is designed to prevent.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 7c58 ACs: 'Each coach move has at least one recall and one no-fire eval case; eval passes before merge.' Add corresponding fixtures to f93a's eval specs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'enriched' in AC6 with a concrete, per-overlay definition of done (e.g. list the named dso calibration items — FP guards, evidence-discipline rules, domain-specific recall patterns — that each overlay must incorporate, matching the specificity of AC5's four named dimensions).
findings.finding: AC6 uses the word 'enriched' with no observable definition of done — an executor cannot determine when the five specialist overlays are sufficiently enriched without stopping to ask what specific content changes are required.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) enriched.'
findings.suggested_fix: For each of the five overlays, name the specific dso content being adopted (e.g. 'security overlay: add the three FP-guard patterns from dso's documented FP incidents; performance overlay: add the scope-boundary and measurement-required guard'). The AC passes when a diff shows those named items present in each overlay prompt.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard inline in AC4 (e.g. 'half 1: test existence/coverage check; half 2: test quality/behavioral-assertion check') so the criterion is self-contained and evaluable without the external gap report.
findings.finding: AC4 references 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard without defining what those two halves are, which an executor cannot resolve without consulting the dso rubric externally.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the Pass-2 output contract (fields, labels, schema) explicitly and describe an expand-contract sequence or rollback path for the verifier prompt upgrade so the deterministic verdict arithmetic layer is not broken by schema drift.
findings.finding: The Pass-2 verifier prompt upgrade (adding contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence checks) may change Pass-2's structured output contract — the fields, labels, or schema that the deterministic verdict arithmetic layer reads. No expand-contract sequence or rollback path is described for this internal interface change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection specifying the Pass-2 structured-output schema (e.g., finding fields, severity vocabulary) as a versioned contract. Ship the new check categories as additive fields only (expand phase), validate the arithmetic layer handles unknown fields gracefully, then remove any deprecated fields in a later contract step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the content-triggered overlay mechanism (new diff-content pattern trigger in overlay_union, or base-reviewer LLM escalation — pick one) and confirm 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' are added to the closed OVERLAY_IDS enum before criteria_routing.json entries are authored.
findings.finding: The plan introduces 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' as two new overlays using a 'content-triggered overlay capability', but neither ID exists in the codebase's closed OVERLAY_IDS enum (registry.py), and the mechanism for content-triggering — distinct from the existing glob-triggered and LLM-escalated paths — is unspecified. Adding new overlay IDs requires coordinated changes to OVERLAY_IDS (enforced by filter_recommend_overlays and overlay_union); omitting this breaks the enum invariant and leaves the overlays unreachable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped; deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' to OVERLAY_IDS in registry.py and specify in the plan whether content-triggering is (a) a new glob-like pattern matching on diff text added to overlay_union, or (b) a convention that the base reviewer detects content signals and escalates via recommend_overlays. The former requires a new overlay_union code path; the latter reuses the existing escalation path with no new mechanism.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a conflict-marker Engine B detector YAML (e.g., an opengrep/ast-grep rule matching `^<<<<<<<`/`^=======`/`^>>>>>>>` lines, or a lightweight metric/regex backend entry under src/rebar/grounding/detectors/builtin/) alongside the criteria_routing.json entry, so the DET criterion has a backing detector that actually fires.
findings.finding: The plan wires a conflict-marker check as a 'DET criterion' in criteria_routing.json, but a DET criterion requires a backing Engine B detector YAML (builtin or project-local). No conflict-marker detector exists in the codebase. Without the detector YAML, det_criteria() returns an entry with detector=None, criterion_for_detector() never matches, and run_detectors() selects zero detectors — the criterion is a silent no-op at runtime.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'conflict-marker deterministic detector wired as an advisory fail-open DET criterion.'
findings.suggested_fix: Author a builtin detector YAML (e.g., `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/builtin/smell_conflict_markers.yaml`) with an opengrep pattern matching `^<{7}` / `^={7}` / `^>{7}` lines, give it the id `rebar.builtin.smell.conflict-markers`, and reference that id in the criteria_routing.json entry's `detector: {id: ...}` field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'prefer AST-LSP' capability assertion in the Pass-2 verifier upgrade AC: specify what AST/LSP tooling is available (or will be added), in which execution mode the verifier would use it, and cite or add a spike/experiment that confirms the capability is achievable within this epic's scope.
findings.finding: The AC 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)' makes an unverified capability assertion: the current Pass-2 verifier for both plan-review and code-review is a single-turn or agentic LLM prompt with no AST or LSP tooling. Whether the verifier can be upgraded to 'prefer AST-LSP' — and what that means concretely (new agentic tools? a new execution mode? a new scripted step?) — is not grounded in the plan or any referenced experiment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define whether 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' are new OVERLAY_IDS entries (requiring OVERLAY_IDS update + drift-guard test update) or plan-review-only overlays, and specify whether 'content-triggered' is a new trigger mechanism or an alias for the existing glob/LLM paths — with the code placement and schema for whichever it is.
findings.finding: 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' are named as overlay IDs in AC3 but are absent from the closed OVERLAY_IDS enum in src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py. The plan also names a 'content-triggered overlay capability' as a new trigger mechanism without defining how it differs from the two existing paths (glob Round-A and LLM-escalated Round-B per ADR 0010). These two overlay IDs are currently unreachable: filter_recommend_overlays() silently drops any id not in OVERLAY_IDS, and overlay_union's (glob ∪ recommend) − already_run formula has no path to include them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped; deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add 'deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent' to OVERLAY_IDS in registry.py and update test_overlay_ids_is_the_expected_closed_set, or (b) clarify these are plan-review-only constructs not touching the code-review OVERLAY_IDS enum. For 'content-triggered', map it explicitly to Round-A (glob pattern) or Round-B (LLM escalation) in the plan text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify for each of contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence whether it becomes a new GRADED_BINARY sub-question (with Pass-3 arithmetic consequences), a new VERIFIER_RULES entry (with test-pin update), or prompt-preamble prose only (no schema change), and update the co-located pinning tests accordingly.
findings.finding: AC5 ('Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded: contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP') introduces four new check types but does not specify their placement relative to the existing verification contract schema. Three load-bearing co-located structures — VERIFIER_RULES (4 entries, pinned by test), GRADED_BINARY (7 fields, pinned by test), and the verification_model schema ({index, analysis, severity_attributes, binary}, pinned by test) — each imply a different placement, each with different Pass-3 arithmetic consequences. Adding new binary sub-questions changes the validity() denominator; adding new VERIFIER_RULES entries breaks the test_scaffold_records_all_four_rules pin; adding prompt-only prose has no structural effect.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'behavioral-testing standard' and its two halves (e.g. as a named document, inline in the AC, or by referencing the dso source) so an executor knows exactly what content the tests overlay must carry to satisfy the AC.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' but neither 'behavioral-testing standard' nor 'both halves' is defined anywhere in the plan or codebase. The current code-review-tests.md is a generic test-sufficiency prompt with no domain-specific standard. An executor cannot determine what the two halves are or when they are satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, and approach-viability note: specify the format/schema, the file or prompt it lives in, the processing protocol (when it is populated, read, and acted on), and the fallback when the sub-step fails or is absent.
findings.finding: AC9 clusters three new output artifacts — 'FP-ledger convention', 'grounding-effort/health telemetry', and 'approach-viability note' — with no schema, no placement in any existing or new prompt/contract file, and no fallback for absent or failed sub-steps. None of these exist anywhere in the codebase. An executor is given the artifact names but no structural specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'depends on f983 (content-trigger seam)' prerequisite to child 3c5d so it cannot be started or merged before f983's seam is unit-tested and available.
findings.finding: Child 3c5d (scope-intent overlay) has an undeclared dependency on child f983 (content-trigger seam): 3c5d says it '(reuses the content-trigger seam)' but lists no prerequisite, meaning 3c5d can be started or merged before f983 ships the seam, causing 3c5d's own AC ('Overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer') to be unachievable.
findings.location: child 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 3c5d's scope or acceptance criteria: 'Prerequisite: f983 content-trigger seam shipped and unit-tested before this overlay's trigger wiring begins.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Establish an explicit ordering constraint: f93a must be merged (with baseline passing) before any other child in this epic that modifies prompt or detector files.
findings.finding: f93a's eval baseline ('specs pass on the current gate as baseline') is invalidated if any prompt-changing child (particularly 4b56) merges before f93a establishes the baseline, yet no ordering constraint is declared between f93a and the prompt stories it is meant to gate.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Add to f93a's AC: 'This story merges before any prompt-changing sibling story; baseline eval green on the unmodified gate is a merge prerequisite for all other stories in this epic.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
